data.narrative:
Eikon Therapeutics is a Series B–stage biotech company founded by Luke Lavis, Eric Betzig, Robert Tjian, and Xavier Darzacq, currently valued at $3.3B post-money and having raised $1.1B+ to date. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of 4 same-sector, same-stage private peers (Neko Health at $1.8B, Chai Discovery at $1.3B, Asimov at $800M, and Profluent at $600M) as well as 3 public Biotech & Health comps (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression modeling applies a typical Series B to next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.9x to the current $3.3B valuation, implying $6.3B. Peer-median analysis across the four same-stage biotech comparables yields a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range of $600M to $1.8B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiple (median 6.7x across 3 tickers) cannot be applied due to absence of revenue disclosure for the private target.

The triangulation of these two methods supports a valuation range of $892M (low, weighted toward peer median) to $7.2B (high, anchored to stage-progression), with a median estimate of $3.7B. The current $3.3B post-money valuation aligns closely with the median outcome. Key uncertainties include the speed and probability of reaching Series C or D milestones (driving stage-progression upside) and whether Eikon's technology differentiation justifies a premium or discount relative to peer cohort medians.
